title: T-114 — Reference Audit Trail screen (SET-054 §8.6)
prompt:
# T-114 — Reference Audit Trail screen (SET-054 §8.6)

PRD: docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md §8.6 (Reference Tables UI surfaces)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Build SET-054 Reference Audit Trail — per-row history viewer with diff between consecutive versions for a single reference_table row. Distinct from SET-013 global audit log (T-079) which is partition-scoped across all entities; this is row-focused with inline diff.

## Implementation contract
1. Page at apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/reference/[table_code]/[row_key]/history/page.tsx (Server Component) reads audit_log entries WHERE table_name='reference_tables' AND entity_id=row_key for caller's org_id, ordered by created_at DESC.
2. List shows: created_at, action (insert|update|delete), user_id, summary of changed columns.
3. Each row expandable to show side-by-side old_value / new_value (re-uses the diff helper from T-108 SET-042 if available; otherwise local recursive walker).
4. Header strip shows: table_code, row_key, current row JSON snapshot (collapsible).
5. RBAC: requires `settings.audit.read` and `settings.reference.read`.

## Files
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/reference/[table_code]/[row_key]/history/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/reference/[table_code]/[row_key]/history/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` array.

## Test strategy
1. RED: RTL test seeds 3 audit_log entries for one reference_tables row (insert + 2 updates); asserts ordered render, expand-to-diff, RBAC 403.
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/reference/[table_code]/[row_key]/history/page.test.tsx`
2. RED must fail.
3. GREEN: implement Server Component + diff render + expand control.
4. Re-run RED + `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not duplicate SET-013 global viewer (T-079); this is row-scoped.
- Do not bypass withOrgContext (RLS).
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (§0).

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. PRD: docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md v3.5.

## Prototype reference
**Source:** `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/admin-screens.jsx:216-344`
**Label:** `rule_detail_screen`
**Component type:** tabs

> **No exact prototype** — closest visual pattern: `rule_detail_screen` at `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/admin-screens.jsx:216-344`. Build in the same structural style as this screen. Re-use the Rule Detail screen's `aud` tab Audit log table (admin-screens.jsx lines 338-345) and `ver` tab expandable rows for the per-row old→new diff. Wrap in the same PageHead + breadcrumb + Section shell.

### Parity requirements (mandatory ACs)
The production implementation MUST match the prototype on three dimensions — add these as explicit ACs:
1. **Structural parity** — same sections, same field labels, same shadcn primitives invoked, same modals triggered from same actions.
2. **Visual parity** — same density tokens, same shadcn/Radix primitives (no drift to plain HTML), same layout proportions.
3. **Interaction parity** — same enable/disable rules, same loading/empty/error states, same keyboard focus order.

### Translation notes (from prototype-labels index)
- Tab state (def/ver/dry/cons/aud) with local useState → shadcn Tabs component; tab id from URL hash for deep-linking
- window.SETTINGS_RULE_DSL / window.SETTINGS_DRY_RUNS → Drizzle queries on rules_dsl and rule_dry_run_results tables
- DSL <pre> read-only display → CodeMirror or Monaco in read-only mode with JSON syntax highlighting
- Version history table (Array.from loop) → real version rows from rules_registry_versions table
- 'Diff vs current' button → Server Action calling /api/rules/:code/diff/:version returning JSON deep-diff
- Dry-run results table → paginated query on rule_dry_run_results; 'View I/O' opens RuleDryRunModal with preloaded result
- openModal('ruleDryRun') → shared Dialog trigger; pass rule and preload props
- Hardcoded labels → next-intl keys

### shadcn primitives to use
`Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`, `Table`, `Badge`, `Button`

pipeline_inputs.description: Reference Audit Trail (SET-054): per-row history viewer with diff between consecutive versions.
pipeline_inputs.details:
PRD §8.6 SET-054: `Per-row history z diff viewer`. Distinct from SET-013 (T-079) which is the global audit log filtered by date partition; this screen drills into a single (table_code, row_key) tuple. Diff helper may import from T-108 if exposed as shared util in `packages/ui` or `apps/web/lib`.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given the caller lacks `settings.audit.read`, when the page is requested, then a 403 page renders.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
out_of_scope: Global audit log (covered by SET-013 / T-079)
Restore-from-history action (defer; current scope is read-only)
acceptance_criteria: Given 3 audit_log entries exist for reference_tables row (table_code='allergens', row_key='gluten') — 1 insert + 2 updates — when the page renders, then 3 history rows display in created_at DESC with action badges.
Given the user expands the most recent update row, when the diff renders, then changed columns show side-by-side old_value vs new_value with added/removed/changed highlighting.
Given the header strip renders, when the row is currently active, then the current snapshot JSON section is collapsible and reflects the latest values.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given the caller lacks `settings.audit.read`, when the page is requested, then a 403 page renders.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: RTL test seeds 3 audit_log entries + asserts list order, expand-to-diff, RBAC 403, header collapsible.
GREEN: implement Server Component + expand control + diff render.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not duplicate SET-013 global viewer (T-079); this is row-scoped.
